books.title: Introduction
books.intro:
In the Name of Allah, the Merciful, the Beneficent - All praise is due to Allah, Lord of the worlds, and the [praiseworthy] end is for those who fear Him; and may Allah send blessings upon Muhammad, seal of the Prophets, and upon all the Prophets and Messengers. As for what follows: Indeed you mentioned, may Allah have mercy on you, by the guidance of your Creator, that you were interested in an examination of what is known of all the transmitted reports on authority of the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings upon him, regarding traditions of the Dīn, its rulings, and everything from it regarding rewards, punishments, motivations, admonishments, and other descriptive topics through chains of narration which were related by and circulated between Ahl ul-Ilm. Thus you wished, may Allah guide you aright, to be informed about all of [the transmitted reports] in the form of a calculated composition and you asked me to abridge [it] for you in writing without a great amount of repetition. You allege that [much repetition] would distract you from what you intended in terms of understanding and deriving rulings from [the reports]. And because of that which you have asked, may Allah be generous with you, when I am attributed to its successful management and whatever condition can be construed by it, if Allah wills, (it will lead to) a praiseworthy ending and obtainable benefit. I thought at the time you asked me to undertake that [task]- if it was determined for me to do so, and preordained for me to complete it- that the first to benefit from that would be me specifically before anyone else and this is due to a great number of reasons which are too lengthy to describe except [to say] that in summary, having precision regarding a select few [narrations], and accuracy in them, is easier for a person than to undertake of a great number of them, and especially for one who is indistinguishable in it from the common people [in this matter] unless someone else informs him of the distinction. If the matter is just as we described, then focusing on the few authentic narrations is worthier for them than seeking an abundance of weak narrations. Although indeed it is hoped for that some benefit is attained by seeking after a large number [of Ḥadīth] of this type, and gathering the repetitions for them, but only for the elite who are endowed with some awareness and knowledge in their means [of ascertaining authenticity] and defects. Thus that, if Allah wills, will happen through whatever will be brought to bear of that [awareness, distinction, knowledge of the means, and defects] on the advantage in seeking large numbers of [the various categories of Ḥadīth]. And as for the common people who are different from the elite in terms of awareness and knowledge, then it is senseless for them to seek large numbers [of various categories of Ḥadīth], while they are unaware of the few [Sahīh]. Then we, if Allah wills, will begin to extract and compose what you have asked upon conditions which we shall mention to you. We set ourselves upon the entirety of what is transmitted from the reports on authority of the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings upon him. Then we divided it up into three sections and three levels of people without repetition except: 1) When coming to a point where it was essential to repeat a narration in which there is an addition that clarifies the meaning of the first one; or

2) When there is a second chain that supports the first one in some hidden defect present since the additional significance in the second Ḥadīth assumes the position of a complete Ḥadīth. Repeating narrations which have the kind of ‘addition’ we described is inevitable [since it eliminates the perceived hidden defect of the first narration]; or that significance [of the addition] is separated from the entire narration by abbreviating it when it is possible [to understand the significance from a small part of the narration], however separating the relevant part of the narration from the rest might make it difficult to understand the link between them, so repeating it in its original form when that proves difficult is safest. Thus when we find that it is avoidable to repeat the narrations in their entirety we take care not to do so, if Allah wills. As for the first category, we aspired to advance the report which is safer from defects than any others, and is purified due to being related by people of integrity in Ḥadīth, and certitude for what they relate; there are no strong disputes found [compared to the reports of other Thiqāt] regarding their transmissions, and no excessive inconsistencies [in their own reports] - just as is the case regarding a great number of Muhaddithīn and which appears in their narrations. Thus when we examined reports of this description from the people, we also came across reports in whose chains there fell some of those who are not described with memorization and precision, like those of the previous description before them. Although they fell below what we described [from the first group], they still have the designation of protection [from ill-repute] and truthfulness; and they acquired knowledge, included among them are the likes of Atā’ bin is-Sā’ib, and Yazīd bin Abī Ziyād, and Layth bin Abī Sulaym, from among the carriers of Āthār and the relaters of Akhbār. So even though they possessed what we described of knowledge, protection and being known as scholars among Ahl ul-Ilm, their contemporaries who we mentioned as precise and sound in transmission were above them in status and rank because this [the first category] is a high rank and sublime characteristic according to Ahl ul-Ilm. Do you not see that when you weigh these three people we mentioned- Atā’, Yazīd, and Layth- with Mansūr bin il-Mu’tamir, Sulaymān al-A’mash and Ismā’īl bin Abī Khālid in regards to precision in Ḥadīth and soundness in it, you will find them distinct from others and not near them [in rank?]- there is no doubt regarding that among the people knowledgeable in Ḥadīth since the soundness of the memorization of Mansūr, al-A’mash, and Ismā’īl, and their precision in Ḥadīth was well-known among [the people knowledgeable in Ḥadīth] and they were not aware of examples of that from Atā’, Yazīd, and Layth. Upon the same course as the above, when you weigh between the two levels like Ibn Awn and Ayyūb as-Sakhtiyānī with Awf ibn Abī Jamīlah and Ash’ath al-Humrānī- and all 4 are companions of al-Hasan and Ibn Sīrīn- there is disparity between the two groups. Between these two groups is a distance in terms of perfection of virtue and soundness of reporting even though Awf and Ash’ath are not repelled from [the status] of truthfulness and honesty according to Ahl ul-Ilm, rather the situation is as we described regarding their position. We only mentioned these examples by way of naming them specifically so that their examples might be an indication for whoever is ignorant of the path to return to understanding of Ahl ul-Ilm regarding the ranking of its people. Thus there is no shortchanging the men of elevated rank any amount of what is due his level, and there is no elevation of those who are lower any amount of knowledge above his position- and each who possesses the right is given his right and is settled in his rank. It has been mentioned on authority of Ā’ishah, may Allah be pleased with her, that she said: ‘The Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings of Allah upon him, ordered us to afford people their (rightful) positions according to what the Qur’ān states: {And above all who possess knowledge is another who is knowledgeable}[Yūsuf: 76] . Thus based on the example of what we mentioned [regarding the narrators of Hifẓ and Itqān, and narrations which lack excessive inconsistency or strong contradiction], we compiled what you asked for of [those kind] of reports on authority of the Messenger of Allah, peace and blessings of Allah upon him. As for anything of that wherein the people were charged [with some criticism] by the people of Ḥadīth, or by the majority of [the people of Ḥadīth], then we did not preoccupy ourselves with bringing forward their narrations, such as Abd Allah ibn Miswar Abī Ja’far il-Madā’inī, Amr bin Khālid, Abd il-Quddūs ash-Shāmī, Muhammad ibn Sa’īd il-Maslūb, Ghiyāth ibn Ibrāhīm, Sulaymān bin Amr Abī Dāwud an-Nakha’ī, and those like them whereof they were accused of fabricating narrations and manufacturing reports; and like that are those whose narrations are dominated with Munkar, or mistakes- we withheld from their narrations as well. An indication of Munkar in the narration of a Muhaddith is when his transmission differs with the transmission of a Muhaddith from the people of memorization and acceptance, or does not agree with it when the two are compared. When the majority of a person’s narrations are like that, he is abandoned [Mahjūr] in Ḥadīth, and not accepted in it, and his narrations are not acted upon. The following are those Muhaddithīn who are among this group: Abd Allah ibn Muharrar, Yahyā bin Abī Unaysah, Al-Jarrāh bin ul-Minhāl Abūl-Atūf, Abbād bin Kathīr, Husayn bin Abd Illah ibn Ḍumayrah, Umar bin Suhbān, and those of the same type in terms of transmission of Munkar Ḥadīth. We did not pause upon their narrations or preoccupy ourselves with them due to the ruling of Ahl ul-Ilm. That which we are aware of from their school of thought in accepting what is singularly reported by a Muhaddith from the narrations is that (the Muhaddith) took part along with the trustworthy narrators from Ahl ul-Ilm wal-Hifẓ in transmitting some of what they transmitted, and [the Muhaddith] is predominantly in agreement with them; when one is found like that, then if he adds to [the transmission] anything not found with his companions, then his addition is accepted. As for those who you see resorting to the likes of az-Zuhrī due to his greatness, and due to the great number of his companions being among the precise Huffāẓ, [resorting to] his Ḥadīth and the Ḥadīth of those like him, or to the likes of Hishām ibn Urwah, then their Ḥadīth are extensively shared among Ahl ul-Ilm. The greater majority of their companions related their Ḥadīth in agreement with one another [with few having contradictions]. Thus to transmit from [Urwah and az-Zuhrī], or one of them, from among the multitude of Ḥadīth, what is not known among any of their companions, and [the Rāwī] is not of those who share in the Sahīh narrations [found] among them, then it is not allowed to accept the narrations of this category of people, and Allah knows best. We have explained from the school of Ḥadīth and its people some of what those who wish to traverse the path of [the Muhaddithīn] should aim for, and be guided towards. We will, if Allah wills, add to the explanation and clarification in another place in this book upon the mention of defective reports [Mu’allalah] when we come to it, in the places where explanation and clarification are appropriate, if Allah wills. And what follows: May Allah have mercy on you, if not for that which we saw of an evil act, largely from those who claim to be Muhaddithīn, in what they were supposed to adhere to when putting forward weak narrations and abominable transmissions and their neglect for the investigation of famous Sahīh narrations related by the trustworthy narrators, well-known for their truthfulness and honesty, after knowledge of them and affirmation with their tongues, that a great many of [weak and abominable narrations] which were cast towards heedless people are denounced and spoken of as not acceptable whereof the A’immah of the people of Ḥadīth criticized their transmissions- A’immah like Mālik ibn Anas, Shu’bat bin al-Hajjāj, Sufyān bin Uyaynah, Yahyā bin Sa’īd al-Qattān, Abd ir-Rahman ibn Mahdī, and other A’immah- then the establishment of what you asked for of distinction [between the types of Ḥadīth] and collection [of those which were Sahīh] would be easy for us. However on account of what we informed you of regarding the people’s circulation of abominable reports with weak, unknown chains, and their casting them towards the common people who are not aware of their defects, responding to what you asked became lighter upon our hearts.
books.notes: This book contains the introductory remarks of Imam Muslim placed at the beginning of his collection. It contains a number of narrations, most of which are nothadithand which may not all meet the strict standards of authenticity required of all hadith in the Sahih. The translation here has been generously provided by Fernando Rodriguez.

chapters.title: Explaining Al-Iman (Faith), Al-Islam, and Al-Ihsan, and The Obligations of Al-Iman With Affirmation of The Qadar of Allah, Glorious And Most High is He. And Explaining The Evidence For Declaring One's Innocence Of One Who Does Not Believe In Al-Qadar, And Having A Harsh View Of His Case
chapters.title_arabic: باب بيان الإيمان والإسلام والإحسان ووجوب الإيمان بإثبات قدر الله سبحانه وتعالى وبيان الدليل على التبري ممن لا يؤمن بالقدر وإغلاظ القول في حقه

chapters.title: What is Al-Iman (Faith)? Explaining Its Characteristics
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِيمَانُ مَا هُوَ وَبَيَانُ خِصَالِهِ

chapters.title: What is Islam? Explaining its characteristics.
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِسْلاَمُ مَا هُوَ وَبَيَانُ خِصَالِهِ ‏

chapters.title: Explaining the prayers which are one of the pillars of Islam
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ الصَّلَوَاتِ الَّتِي هِيَ أَحَدُ أَرْكَانِ الإِسْلاَمِ ‏

chapters.title: Asking about the pillars of Islam
chapters.title_arabic: باب السُّؤَالِ عَنْ أَرْكَانِ الْإِسْلَامِ

chapters.title: Explaining the faith by means of which a person is admitted into paradise, and that the one who adheres to what is enjoined upon him will enter paradise
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ الإِيمَانِ الَّذِي يُدْخَلُ بِهِ الْجَنَّةُ وَأَنَّ مَنْ تَمَسَّكَ بِمَا أُمِرَ بِهِ دَخَلَ الجَنَّةَ

chapters.title: Clarifying the pillars of Islam and its grand supports
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ أَرْكَانِ الْإِسْلَامِ وَدَعَائِمِهِ الْعِظَامِ

chapters.title: The command to believe in Allah and His Messenger (saws) and the laws of Islam, calling people to it, asking about it, memorizing it and conveying it to those who have not heard the message
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْأَمْرِ بِالْإِيمَانِ بِاللَّهِ تَعَالَى وَرَسُولِهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ وَشَرَائِعِ الدِّينِ

chapters.title: Calling people to the twin declaration of faith and the laws of Islam
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ إِلَى الشَّهَادَتَيْنِ وَشَرَائِعِ الإِسْلاَمِ

chapters.title: The command to fight the people until they say "La ilaha illallah Muhammad Rasul-Allah", and establish Salat, and pay the Zakat, and believe in everything that the prophet (saws) brought. Whoever does that, his life and his wealth are protected except by its right, and his secrets are entrusted to Allah, the most high. Fighting those who withhold Zakat or other than that is one of the duties of Islam and the Imam should be concerned with the Laws of Islam
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَمْرِ بِقِتَالِ النَّاسِ حَتَّى يَقُولُوا لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ مُحَمَّدٌ رَسُولُ اللَّهِ ‏‏ويقيموا الصلاة ويؤتوا الزكاة ويؤمنوا بجميع ما جاء به النبي صلى الله عليه وسلم وأن من فعل ذلك عصم نفسه وماله إلا بحقها ووكلت سريرته إلى الله تعالى وقتال من منع الزكاة أو غيرها من حقوق الإسلام واهتمام الإمام بشعائر الإسلام

chapters.title: Evidence that the islam of one who becomes muslim on his deathbed is valid, so long as the death thoes have not begun; Abrogation of permission to supplicate for forgiveness for the idolators; Evidence that one who dies an idolator is one of the people of hell and no intervention can save him from that
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدليل على صحة إسلام من حضره الموت ما لم يشرع في النزع وهو الغرغرة ونسخ جواز الاستغفار للمشركين والدليل على أن من مات على الشرك فهو في أصحاب الجحيم ولا ينقذه من ذلك شيء من الوسائل

chapters.title: The evidence that one who dies believing in tawhid will definitely enter paradise
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ لَقِيَ اللَّهَ بِالإِيمَانِ وَهُوَ غَيْرُ شَاكٍّ فِيهِ دَخَلَ الْجَنَّةَ وَحَرُمَ عَلَى النَّارِ

chapters.title: Evidence that the one who is content with Allah as his Lord, Islam as his religion and Muhammad (saws) as his prophet, then he is a believer, even if he commits major sins
chapters.title_arabic: بَاب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ مَنْ رَضِيَ بِاللَّهِ رَبًّا وَبِالْإِسْلَامِ دِينًا وَبِمُحَمَّدٍ رَسُولًا فَهُوَ مُؤْمِنٌ وَإِنْ ارْتَكَبَ الْمَعَاصِيَ الْكَبَائِرَ

chapters.title: Clarifying the number of branches of faith, the best and the least of them, the virtue of modesty (Al-Haya') and the fact that it is part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ عَدَدِ شُعَبِ الْإِيمَانِ وَأَفْضَلِهَا وَأَدْنَاهَا وَفَضِيلَةِ الْحَيَاءِ وَكَوْنِهِ مِنْ الْإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: A phrase that sums up Islam
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَامِعِ أَوْصَافِ الإِسْلاَمِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the superiority of Islam, and what part of it is best
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ تَفَاضُلِ الإِسْلاَمِ وَأَىِّ أُمُورِهِ أَفْضَلُ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarification of those characteristics which, if a person attains them, he will find the sweetness of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ خِصَالٍ مَنِ اتَّصَفَ بِهِنَّ وَجَدَ حَلاَوَةَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: The obligation to love the messenger of Allah (saws) more than one's family, son, father, and all other people; And mentions of an absolute absence of faith regarding one who does not love him with such love
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ مَحَبَّةِ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ أَكْثَرَ مِنَ الأَهْلِ وَالْوَلَدِ وَالْوَالِدِ وَالنَّاسِ أَجْمَعِينَ وَإِطْلاَقِ عَدَمِ الإِيمَانِ عَلَى مَنْ لَمْ يُحِبَّهُ هَذِهِ الْمَحَبَّةَ

chapters.title: The evidence that one of the attributes of faith is to love for one's brother muslim that one loves for oneself of goodness
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ مِنْ خِصَالِ الإِيمَانِ أَنْ يُحِبَّ لأَخِيهِ الْمُسْلِمِ مَا يُحِبُّ لِنَفْسِه مِنَ الْخَيْرِ

chapters.title: Clarifying the prohibition of annoying one's neighbor
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ تَحْرِيمِ إِيذَاءِ الْجَارِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Encouragement to honor one's neighbor and guest, and the obligation to remain silent unless one has something good to say, and the fact that all of that is part of faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَثِّ عَلَى إِكْرَامِ الْجَارِ وَالضَّيْفِ وَلُزُومِ الصَّمْتِ إِلاَّ مِنَ الْخَيْرِ وَكَوْنِ ذَلِكَ كُلِّهِ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: Clarifying that forbidding evil is part of faith, faith increases and decreases; Enjoying what is good and forbidding what is evil are obligatory
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ كَوْنِ النَّهْىِ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ، مِنَ الإِيمَانِ وَأَنَّ الإِيمَانَ يَزِيدُ وَيَنْقُصُ وَأَنَّ الأَمْرَ بِالْمَعْرُوفِ وَالنَّهْيَ عَنِ الْمُنْكَرِ وَاجِبَانِ

chapters.title: People excel over one another in faith, and the superiority of the people of Yemen in faith
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَفَاضُلِ أَهْلِ الإِيمَانِ فِيهِ وَرُجْحَانِ أَهْلِ الْيَمَنِ فِيهِ ‏

chapters.title: Clarifying that no one will enter paradise but the believers; loving the believers is part of faith and spreading Salam is a means of attaining that
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ أَنَّهُ لاَ يَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ إِلاَّ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ وَأَنَّ مَحَبَّةَ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ وَأَنَّ إِفْشَاءَ السَّلاَمِ سَبَبٌ لِحُصُولِهَا

chapters.title: Clarifying that the religion is sincerity
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ أَنَّ الدِّينَ النَّصِيحَةُ

chapters.title: Clarifying that faith decreases because of disobedience and negating it from the one committing the act of disobedience, with the meaning of negating its completion
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ نُقْصَانِ الإِيمَانِ بِالْمَعَاصِي وَنَفْيِهِ عَنِ الْمُتَلَبِّسِ بِالْمَعْصِيَةِ عَلَى إِرَادَةِ نَفْيِ كَمَالِهِ

chapters.title: The characteristics of the hypocrite
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ خِصَالِ الْمُنَافِقِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the condition of faith for one who says to his muslim brother: "O Kafir (Disbeliever)."
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ حَالِ إِيمَانِ مَنْ قَالَ لأَخِيهِ الْمُسْلِمِ يَا كَافِرُ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the condition of the fath of one who knowingly denies his father
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ حَالِ إِيمَانِ مَنْ رَغِبَ عَنْ أَبِيهِ وَهُوَ يَعْلَمُ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the words of the prophet (saws): "Insulting A Muslim is an evil action and fighting him is disbelief (Kufr)"
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏"‏ سِبَابُ الْمُسْلِمِ فُسُوقٌ وَقِتَالُهُ كُفْرٌ ‏"‏ ‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the meaning of the Statement of the Prophet (saws): "Do not revert to disbelief (Kuffar) after I am Gone, striking one another's necks"
chapters.title_arabic: باب ‏"‏ لاَ تَرْجِعُوا بَعْدِي كُفَّارًا يَضْرِبُ بَعْضُكمْ رِقَابَ بَعْضٍ ‏"‏ ‏‏

chapters.title: Use of the word Kufr with regard to slandering people's lineage and wailing
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِطْلاَقِ اسْمِ الْكُفْرِ عَلَى الطَّعْنِ فِي النَّسَبِ وَالنِّيَاحَةِ عَلَى الْمَيِّتِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Calling a runaway slave a kafirg
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَسْمِيَةِ الْعَبْدِ الآبِقِ كَافِرًا ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the Kufr of one who says: "We got rain because of the stars."
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ كُفْرِ مَنْ قَالَ مُطِرْنَا بِالنَّوْءِ ‏

chapters.title: Evidence that love of the Ansar and Ali (r.a.) is a part of faith and a sign thereof; Hating them is a sign of hypocrisy
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ حُبَّ الأَنْصَارِ وَعَلِيٍّ رَضِيَ اللَّهُ عَنْهُمْ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ وَعَلاَمَاتِهِ وَبُغْضَهُمْ مِنْ عَلاَمَاتِ النِّفَاقِ

chapters.title: Clarifying that faith decreases with shortcoming in obedience, and the word Kufr may be used with regard to matters other than dsbelief in Allah, such as ingratitude for blessings and not fulfilling one's duties
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ نُقْصَانِ الإِيمَانِ بِنَقْصِ الطَّاعَاتِ وَبَيَانِ إِطْلاَقِ لَفْظِ الْكُفْرِ عَلَى غَيْرِ الْكُفْرِ بِاللَّهِ كَكُفْرِ النِّعْمَةِ وَالْحُقُوقِ

chapters.title: Clarifying the usage of the word Kafir for one who abandons Salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ إِطْلاَقِ اسْمِ الْكُفْرِ عَلَى مَنْ تَرَكَ الصَّلاَةَ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying that faith in Allah most high is the best of deeds
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ كَوْنِ الإِيمَانِ بِاللَّهِ تَعَالَى أَفْضَلَ الأَعْمَالِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying that Shirk is the worst of sins, and the worst sins after Shirk
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَوْنِ الشِّرْكِ أَقْبَحَ الذُّنُوبِ وَبَيَانِ أَعْظَمِهَا بَعْدَهُ ‏‏

chapters.title: The major sins and the most serious of them
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ الْكَبَائِرِ وَأَكْبَرِهَا ‏‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of pride and definition of it
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْرِيمِ الْكِبْرِ وَبَيَانِهِ ‏

chapters.title: The evidence that the one who dies not associating anything with Allah will enter paradise, and the one who dies as idolator will enter the fire
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ مَاتَ لاَ يُشْرِكُ بِاللَّهِ شَيْئًا دَخَلَ الْجَنَّةَ وَمَنْ مَاتَ مُشْرِكًا دَخَلَ النَّارَ ‏‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of killing a disbeliever after he says La ilaha illallah
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْرِيمِ قَتْلِ الْكَافِرِ بَعْدَ أَنْ قَالَ لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ ‏‏

chapters.title: The Saying of the prophet (saws): "Whoever bears weapons against us is not one of us."
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ - صلى الله تعالى عليه وسلم - ‏"‏ مَنْ حَمَل عَلَيْنَا السِّلاَحَ فَلَيْسَ مِنَّا ‏"‏ ‏

chapters.title: The Saying of the prophet (saws): "Whoever deceives us is not one of us."
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ - صلى الله تعالى عليه وسلم - ‏"‏ مَنْ غَشَّنَا فَلَيْسَ مِنَّا ‏"‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of striking one's checks, tearing one's garment and calling with the calls of Jahilliyyah
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْرِيمِ ضَرْبِ الْخُدُودِ وَشَقِّ الْجُيُوبِ وَالدُّعَاءِ بِدَعْوَى الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the emphatic prohibition of An-Namimah (Malicious Gossip)
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ غِلَظِ تَحْرِيمِ النَّمِيمَةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the emphatic prohibition of letting one's garment hang below the ankles (isbal), reminding others of one's gift and selling goods by means of a false oath; Mention of the three to whom Allah, Most High, will not speak on the day of resurrection, nor look at them, nor sanctify them, and theirs will be a painful torment
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ غِلَظِ تَحْرِيمِ إِسْبَالِ الإِزَارِ وَالْمَنِّ بِالْعَطِيَّةِ وَتَنْفِيقِ السِّلْعَةِ بِالْحَلِفِ وَبَيَانِ الثَّلاَثَةِ الَّذِينَ لاَ يُكَلِّمُهُمُ اللَّهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيَامَةِ وَلاَ يَنْظُرُ إِلَيْهِمْ وَلاَ يُزَكِّيهِمْ وَلَهُمْ عَذَابٌ أَلِيمٌ

chapters.title: Clarifying the emphatic prohibition against killing oneself; The one who kills himself with something will be punished with it in the fire; And that no one will enter Paradise but a muslim
chapters.title_arabic: باب غِلَظِ تَحْرِيمِ قَتْلِ الإِنْسَانِ نَفْسَهُ وَإِنَّ مَنْ قَتَلَ نَفْسَهُ بِشَيْءٍ عُذِّبَ بِهِ فِي النَّارِ وَأَنَّهُ لاَ يَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ إِلاَّ نَفْسٌ مُسْلِمَةٌ

chapters.title: Emphatic prohibition against stealing from the spoils of war; and that no one will enter paradise except the believers
chapters.title_arabic: باب غِلَظِ تَحْرِيمِ الْغُلُولِ وَأَنَّهُ لاَ يَدْخُلُ الْجَنَّةَ إِلاَّ الْمُؤْمِنُونَ ‏‏

chapters.title: The evidence that the one who kills himself is not considered a disbeliever
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ قَاتِلَ نَفْسِهِ لاَ يَكْفُرُ

chapters.title: Regarding the wind which will come just before the resurrection and take the soul of anyone who has any fath in his heart
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي الرِّيحِ الَّتِي تَكُونُ قُرْبَ الْقِيَامَةِ تَقْبِضُ مَنْ فِي قَلْبِهِ شَىْءٌ مِنَ الإِيمَانِ

chapters.title: Encouragement to hasten to do good deeds before the emergence of the Fitnah
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْحَثِّ عَلَى الْمُبَادَرَةِ بِالأَعْمَالِ قَبْلَ تَظَاهُرِ الْفِتَنِ

chapters.title: The believer's fear that his good deeds may be lost
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَخَافَةِ الْمُؤْمِنِ أَنْ يَحْبَطَ عَمَلُهُ

chapters.title: Will a person be punished for his actions during the Jahlilliyyah?
chapters.title_arabic: باب هَلْ يُؤَاخَذُ بِأَعْمَالِ الْجَاهِلِيَّةِ

chapters.title: Islam destroys that which came before it, as do Hijrah (Emigration) and Hajj
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَوْنِ الإِسْلاَمِ يَهْدِمُ مَا قَبْلَهُ وَكَذَا الْهِجْرَةُ وَالْحَجُّ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the ruling of the action of an unbeliever if he accepts Islam after it
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ حُكْمِ عَمَلِ الْكَافِرِ إِذَا أَسْلَمَ بَعْدَهُ ‏‏

chapters.title: Sincerity of Faith and its Purity
chapters.title_arabic: باب صِدْقِ الإِيمَانِ وَإِخْلاَصِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarification that Allah, most high allows a person's thoughts and whatever occurs in his heart, so long as they do not become established, and the clarification that he, glorious is he and most high, does not burden anyone with more than he can bear, and clarifying the ruling on thinking of doing good and bad deeds
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ أَنَّهُ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى لَمْ يُكَلِّفْ إِلَّا مَا يُطَاقُ

chapters.title: Allah allows a person's thoughts and whatever occurs in his heart so long as they do not become established
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَجَاوُزِ اللَّهِ عَنْ حَدِيثِ النَّفْسِ، وَالْخَوَاطِرِ، بِالْقَلْبِ إِذَا لَمْ تَسْتَقِرَّ ‏‏

chapters.title: If a person thinks of doing a good deed it will be recorded for him, and if he thinks of doing a bad deed it will not be recorded for him
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِذَا هَمَّ الْعَبْدُ بِحَسَنَةٍ كُتِبَتْ وَإِذَا هَمَّ بِسَيِّئَةٍ لَمْ تُكْتَبْ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying the Waswasah (Whispers, Bad Thoughts) with regard to faith, and what the one who experiences that should say
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ الْوَسْوَسَةِ فِي الإِيمَانِ وَمَا يَقُولُهُ مَنْ وَجَدَهَا ‏‏

chapters.title: Warning of the Fire for the one who swears a false oath in order to unlawfully take the right of another muslim
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَعِيدِ مَنِ اقْتَطَعَ حَقَّ مُسْلِمٍ بِيَمِينٍ فَاجِرَةٍ بِالنَّارِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The evidence that the blood of one who aims to seize other people's wealth without right may be shed, if he is killed he will be in the fire, and the one who is killed defending his property is a martyr
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ مَنْ قَصَدَ أَخْذَ مَالِ غَيْرِهِ بِغَيْرِ حَقٍّ كَانَ الْقَاصِدُ مُهْدَرَ الدَّمِ فِي حَقِّهِ وَإِنْ قُتِلَ كَانَ فِي النَّارِ وَأَنَّ مَنْ قُتِلَ دُونَ مَالِهِ فَهُوَ شَهِيدٌ

chapters.title: One in charge of a matter, who cheats his subjects, deserves the fire
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْقَاقِ الْوَالِي الْغَاشِّ لِرَعِيَّتِهِ النَّارَ ‏‏

chapters.title: The disappearance of honesty and faith from some hearts and the appearance of fitnah in some hearts
chapters.title_arabic: باب رَفْعِ الأَمَانَةِ وَالإِيمَانِ مِنْ بَعْضِ الْقُلُوبِ وَعَرْضِ الْفِتَنِ عَلَى الْقُلُوبِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying that Islam started as something strange, and will revert to being something strange, and it will retreat between the two Masajid
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ أَنَّ الْإِسْلَامَ بَدَأَ غَرِيبًا وَسَيَعُودُ غَرِيبًا وَأَنَّهُ يَأْرِزُ بَيْنَ الْمَسْجِدَيْنِ

chapters.title: The Disappearance of Faith at the End of Time
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذَهَابِ الإِيمَانِ آخِرَ الزَّمَانِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Permissibility of concealing one's faith in the case of fear
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ الاِسْتِسْرَارِ لِلْخَائِفِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Being kind to one for whose faith there is concern because it is weak; Prohibition of attributing Faith to someone without definitive evidence
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَأَلُّفِ قَلْبِ مَنْ يَخَافُ عَلَى إِيمَانِهِ لِضَعْفِهِ وَالنَّهْيِ عَنِ الْقَطْعِ بِالإِيمَانِ مِنْ غَيْرِ دَلِيلٍ قَاطِعٍ

chapters.title: Increasing the heart's tranquility with the appearance evidence
chapters.title_arabic: باب زِيَادَةِ طُمَأْنِينَةِ الْقَلْبِ بِتَظَاهُرِ الأَدِلَّةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Obligation of believing that the message of our Prophet Muhammad (saws) is for all people, and the abrogation of all other religions
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ الإِيمَانِ بِرِسَالَةِ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ إِلَى جَمِيعِ النَّاسِ وَنَسْخِ الْمِلَلِ بِمِلَّتِهِ

chapters.title: The descent of 'Eisa bin Mariam to judge according to the Shari'ah of our Prophet Muhammad (saws); And how Allah has honored this Ummah; And clarifying the evidence that this religion will not be abrogated; and that a group from it will continue to adhere to the truth and prevail until the day of resurrection
chapters.title_arabic: باب نُزُولِ عِيسَى ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ حَاكِمًا بِشَرِيعَةِ نَبِيِّنَا مُحَمَّدٍ صلى الله عليه وسلم

chapters.title: Clarifying the time when faith will no longer be accepted
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ الزَّمَنِ الَّذِي لاَ يُقْبَلُ فِيهِ الإِيمَانُ ‏‏

chapters.title: The beginning of the revelation to the messenger of Allah (saws)
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَدْءِ الْوَحْىِ إِلَى رَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏‏

chapters.title: The night journey on which the messenger of Allah (saws) was taken up into the heavens and the prayers were enjoined
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِسْرَاءِ بِرَسُولِ اللَّهِ صلى الله عليه وسلم إِلَى السَّمَوَاتِ وَفَرْضِ الصَّلَوَاتِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Mentioning Al-Masih Son of Mariam and Al-Masih ad-Dajjal
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي ذِكْرِ الْمَسِيحِ ابْنِ مَرْيَمَ وَالْمَسِيحِ الدَّجَّالِ ‏‏

chapters.title: About (The Lote-Tree of the Utmost Boundary)
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي ذِكْرِ سِدْرَةِ الْمُنْتَهَى ‏‏

chapters.title: The Meaning of the Saying of Allah, The Mighty and Sublime: And indeed he saw him at a second descent (another time)"; And did the prophet (saws) see his lord on the night of the Isra?
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَعْنَى قَوْلِ اللَّهِ عَزَّ وَجَلَّ: {وَلَقَدْ رَآهُ نَزْلَةً أُخْرَى} وَهَلْ رَأَى النَّبِيُّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ رَبَّهُ لَيْلَةَ الإِسْرَاءِ

chapters.title: The saying of the Prophet (saws): "Light, How could I see Him?" and: "I saw Light"
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي قَوْلِهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمُ ‏"‏ نُورٌ أَنَّى أَرَاهُ ‏"‏ ‏.‏ وَفِي قَوْلِهِ ‏"‏ رَأَيْتُ نُورًا

chapters.title: The saying of the Prophet (saws): "Allah does not sleep" and "His veil is light, and if he were to remove it, the splendour of his face would burn all of his creation, as far as hs sght reaches".
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي قَوْلِهِ عَلَيْهِ السَّلاَمُ: «إِنَّ اللَّهَ لاَ يَنَامُ». وَفِي قَوْلِهِ: «حِجَابُهُ النُّورُ لَوْ كَشَفَهُ لأَحْرَقَ سُبُحَاتُ وَجْهِهِ مَا انْتَهَى إِلَيْهِ بَصَرُهُ مِنْ خَلْقِهِ»

chapters.title: Affirming that the believers will see their Lord, Glorious is he and most high, in the hereafter
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْبَاتِ رُؤْيَةِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ فِي الآخِرَةِ رَبَّهُمْ سُبْحَانَهُ وَتَعَالَى ‏‏

chapters.title: Knowing about the seeing
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَعْرِفَةِ طَرِيقِ الرُّؤْيَةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Intercession and bringing those who believed in tawhid out of the Fire
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْبَاتِ الشَّفَاعَةِ وَإِخْرَاجِ الْمُوَحِّدِينَ مِنَ النَّارِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The Last of the people of the Fire to be brought out of it
chapters.title_arabic: باب آخِرِ أَهْلِ النَّارِ خُرُوجًا ‏‏

chapters.title: The Status of the Lowest people in paradise
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَدْنَى أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ مَنْزِلَةً فِيهَا ‏‏

chapters.title: Regarding the saying of the Prophet (saws): "I will be the first of the people to intercede concerning Paradise, and I will be the Prophet with the Greatest Number of Followers."
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي قَوْلِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ: «أَنَا أَوَّلُ النَّاسِ يَشْفَعُ فِي الْجَنَّةِ وَأَنَا أَكْثَرُ الأَنْبِيَاءِ تَبَعًا»

chapters.title: The Prophet (saws) will defer his supplication in order to intercede for his ummah
chapters.title_arabic: باب اخْتِبَاءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم دَعْوَةَ الشَّفَاعَةِ لأُمَّتِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The supplication of the Prophet (saws) for his ummah and his weeping out of compassion for them
chapters.title_arabic: باب دُعَاءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم لأُمَّتِهِ وَبُكَائِهِ شَفَقَةً عَلَيْهِمْ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying that whoever died upon disbelief then he is in the Fire, and no intercession or relationship with those who are close to Allah will be of any avail for him
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ أَنَّ مَنْ مَاتَ عَلَى الْكُفْرِ فَهُوَ فِي النَّارِ وَلاَ تَنَالُهُ شَفَاعَةٌ وَلاَ تَنْفَعُهُ قَرَابَةُ الْمُقَرَّبِينَ

chapters.title: Regarding the saying of Allah, the most High: "And warn your tribe of near kindred."
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي قَوْلِهِ تَعَالَى: {وَأَنْذِرْ عَشِيرَتَكَ الأَقْرَبِينَ}

chapters.title: The Intercession of the Prophet (saws) for Abu Talib and the reduction of his punishment as a result
chapters.title_arabic: باب شَفَاعَةِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم لأَبِي طَالِبٍ وَالتَّخْفِيفِ عَنْهُ بِسَبَبِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The least severely punished of the people of the Fire
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَهْوَنِ أَهْلِ النَّارِ عَذَابًا ‏‏

chapters.title: The evidence that whoever dies upon disbelief, no good deed will benefit him
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ علَى أَنَّ مَنْ مَاتَ عَلَى الْكُفْرِ لاَ يَنْفَعُهُ عَمَلٌ ‏‏

chapters.title: Allegiance to the believers, and forsaking others and disavowing them
chapters.title_arabic: باب مُوَالاَةِ الْمُؤْمِنِينَ وَمُقَاطَعَةِ غَيْرِهِمْ وَالْبَرَاءَةِ مِنْهُمْ ‏‏

chapters.title: The Evidence that groups of Muslims will enter Paradise without being called to account, and without being punished
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى دُخُولِ طَوَائِفَ مِنَ الْمُسْلِمِينَ الْجَنَّةَ بِغَيْرِ حِسَابٍ وَلاَ عَذَابٍ ‏‏

chapters.title: Clarifying that this ummah will form half of the People of Paradise
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَوْنِ هَذِهِ الأُمَّةِ نِصْفَ أَهْلِ الْجَنَّةِ ‏

chapters.title: Allah will say to Adam: "Bring out the Portion of the Fire; Nine Hundred and Ninety-Nine Out of Every Thousand."
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَوْلِهِ يَقُولُ اللَّهُ لِآدَمَ أَخْرِجْ بَعْثَ النَّارِ مِنْ كُلِّ أَلْفٍ تِسْعَ مِائَةٍ وَتِسْعَةً وَتِسْعِينَ

chapters.title: The virtue of wudu’
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْوُضُوءِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The obligation of purifying oneself for the salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ الطَّهَارَةِ لِلصَّلاَةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The description of wudu’ and its perfection
chapters.title_arabic: باب صِفَةِ الْوُضُوءِ وَكَمَالِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The virtue of performing wudu’ and salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْوُضُوءِ وَالصَّلاَةِ عَقِبَهُ ‏‏

chapters.title: The five daily prayers, from one Jumu`ah to the next, and from one Ramadan to the next, are an expiation for whatever (sins) come in between, so long as one avoids major sins
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلَوَاتُ الْخَمْسُ وَالْجُمُعَةُ إِلَى الْجُمُعَةِ وَرَمَضَانُ إِلَى رَمَضَانَ مُكَفِّرَاتٌ لِمَا بَيْنَهُنَّ مَا اجْتُنِبَتِ الْكَبَائِرُ.

chapters.title: Adh-Dhikr (the remembrance) which is recommended following wudu’
chapters.title_arabic: باب الذِّكْرِ الْمُسْتَحَبِّ عَقِبَ الْوُضُوءِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Another description of wudu’
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي وُضُوءِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏‏

chapters.title: Odd numbers when rinsing the nose and cleaning oneself with pebbles (istijmar)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِيتَارِ فِي الاِسْتِنْثَارِ وَالاِسْتِجْمَارِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The obligation of washing the feet completely
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ غَسْلِ الرِّجْلَيْنِ بِكَمَالِهِمَا ‏‏

chapters.title: The obligation of completely washing all the parts to be washed when purifying oneself
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ اسْتِيعَابِ جَمِيعِ أَجْزَاءِ مَحَلِّ الطَّهَارَةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Sins exit with the water of wudu’
chapters.title_arabic: باب خُرُوجِ الْخَطَايَا مَعَ مَاءِ الْوُضُوءِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The recommendation to increase the area washed for the forehead, arms, and legs well when performing wudu’
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ إِطَالَةِ الْغُرَّةِ وَالتَّحْجِيلِ فِي الْوُضُوءِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Adornment (in the hereafter) will reach as far as the wudu’ reached
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَبْلُغُ الْحِلْيَةُ حَيْث يَبْلُغُ الْوَضُوءُ ‏‏

chapters.title: The virtue of isbaghil-wudu’ (performing wudu’ properly) during times when it is difficult to do so
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ إِسْبَاغِ الْوُضُوءِ عَلَى الْمَكَارِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Siwak (tooth-stick)
chapters.title_arabic: باب السِّوَاكِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The characteristics of the fitrah
chapters.title_arabic: باب خِصَالِ الْفِطْرَةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Cleaning oneself after relieving oneself
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِطَابَةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of cleaning oneself with the right hand
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنْ الاِسْتِنْجَاءِ، بِالْيَمِينِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Starting on the right when purifying oneself and in other matters
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّيَمُّنِ فِي الطُّهُورِ وَغَيْرِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of relieving oneself in the street or in the shade
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنِ التَّخَلِّي، فِي الطُّرُقِ وَالظِّلاَلِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Cleaning oneself with water after defecating
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِسْتِنْجَاءِ بِالْمَاءِ مِنَ التَّبَرُّزِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Wiping over the khuff (leather socks)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَسْحِ عَلَى الْخُفَّيْنِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Wiping over the forehead and the imamah
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَسْحِ عَلَى النَّاصِيَةِ وَالْعِمَامَةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Time-limit for wiping over the khuff
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّوْقِيتِ فِي الْمَسْحِ عَلَى الْخُفَّيْنِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The permissibility of performing all the prayers with one wudu’
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ الصَّلَوَاتِ كُلِّهَا بِوُضُوءٍ وَاحِدٍ ‏‏

chapters.title: It is disliked for the person who wants to perform wudu’, and others, to put his hand in the vessel (containing water) before washing it three times, if he is not sure whether something impure is on his hands or not
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهَةِ غَمْسِ الْمُتَوَضِّئِ وَغَيْرِهِ يَدَهُ الْمَشْكُوكَ فِي نَجَاسَتِهَا فِي الإِنَاءِ قَبْلَ غَسْلِهَا ثَلاَثًا

chapters.title: Rulings on what was licked by a dog
chapters.title_arabic: باب حُكْمِ وُلُوغِ الْكَلْبِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of urinating into standing water
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنِ الْبَوْلِ، فِي الْمَاءِ الرَّاكِدِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Prohibition of performing ghusl in standing water
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنْ الاِغْتِسَالِ، فِي الْمَاءِ الرَّاكِدِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The obligation to wash away urine and other impurities if they result in the Masjid, and the ground may be purified with water with no need to scrub it
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ غَسْلِ الْبَوْلِ وَغَيْرِهِ مِنَ النَّجَاسَاتِ إِذَا حَصُلَتْ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ وَأَنَّ الأَرْضَ تَطْهُرُ بِالْمَاءِ مِنْ غَيْرِ حَاجَةٍ إِلَى حَفْرِهَا

chapters.title: The ruling on the urine of a nursing infant and how to wash it
chapters.title_arabic: باب حُكْمِ بَوْلِ الطِّفْلِ الرَّضِيعِ وَكَيْفِيَّةِ غَسْلِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The ruling on semen
chapters.title_arabic: باب حكْمِ الْمَنِيِّ ‏

chapters.title: The impurity of blood and how to wash it
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَجَاسَةِ الدَّمِ وَكَيْفِيَّةِ غَسْلِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The evidence that urine is impure and the obligation to take precautions concerning it
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى نَجَاسَةِ الْبَوْلِ وَوُجُوبِ الاِسْتِبْرَاءِ مِنْهُ ‏‏

chapters.title: Touching a menstruating woman above the izar (waist wrapper)
chapters.title_arabic: باب مُبَاشَرَةِ الْحَائِضِ فَوْقَ الإِزَارِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Lying down with a menstruating woman under a single cover
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِضْطِجَاعِ مَعَ الْحَائِضِ فِي لِحَافٍ وَاحِدٍ ‏‏

chapters.title: It is permissible for a menstruating woman to wash her husband’s head, and comb his hair; her leftovers are pure (tahir); and regarding reclining in her lap and reciting Qur’an
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ غَسلِ الْحَائِضِ رَأْسَ زَوْجِهَا وَتَرْجِيلِهِ وَطَهَارَةِ سُؤْرِهَا وَالاِتِّكَاءِ فِي حِجْرِهَا وَقِرَاءَةِ الْقُرْآنِ فِيهِ

chapters.title: Madhi (prostatic fluid)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَذْىِ ‏

chapters.title: Washing the face and hands when waking from sleep
chapters.title_arabic: باب غَسْلِ الْوَجْهِ وَالْيَدَيْنِ إِذَا اسْتَيْقَظَ مِنَ النَّوْمِ ‏‏

chapters.title: It is permissible for one who is junub to sleep, but it is recommended for him to perform wudu’, and wash his private parts if he wants to eat, drink, sleep, or have intercourse
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ نَوْمِ الْجُنُبِ وَاسْتِحْبَابِ الْوُضُوءِ لَهُ وَغَسْلِ الْفَرْجِ إِذَا أَرَادَ أَنْ يَأْكُلَ أَوْ يَشْرَبَ أَوْ يَنَامَ أَوْ يُجَامِعَل

chapters.title: Women are obliged to perform ghusl if they emit fluid
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ الْغُسْلِ عَلَى الْمَرْأَةِ بِخُرُوجِ الْمَنِيِّ مِنْهَا ‏‏

chapters.title: Description of the (fluid) of the man and the woman; the child is created from the water of both of them
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ صِفَةِ مَنِيِّ الرَّجُلِ وَالْمَرْأَةِ وَأَنَّ الْوَلَدَ مَخْلُوقٌ مِنْ مَائِهِمَا ‏‏

chapters.title: Description of ghusl in the case of janabah (sexual impurity)
chapters.title_arabic: باب صِفَةِ غُسْلِ الْجَنَابَةِ ‏

chapters.title: The amount of water with which it is recommended to perform ghusl in the case of janabah; a man and woman washing from a single vessel; one of them washing with the left-over water of the other
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْقَدْرِ الْمُسْتَحَبِّ مِنَ الْمَاءِ فِي غُسْلِ الْجَنَابَةِ وَغُسْلِ الرَّجُلِ وَالْمَرْأَةِ فِي إِنَاءٍ وَاحِدٍ فِي حَالَةٍ وَاحِدَةٍ وَغُسْلِ أَحَدِهِمَا بِفَضْلِ الآخَرِ

chapters.title: It is recommended to pour water over the head, and elsewhere, three times
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ إِفَاضَةِ الْمَاءِ عَلَى الرَّأْسِ وَغَيْرِهِ ثَلاَثًا ‏

chapters.title: Ruling on the braids of a woman who is doing ghusl
chapters.title_arabic: باب حُكْمِ ضَفَائِرِ الْمُغْتَسِلَةِ ‏

chapters.title: It is recommended for the woman who is performing ghusl following menses to apply a piece of cloth scented with musk to the site of the bleeding
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ اسْتِعْمَالِ الْمُغْتَسِلَةِ مِنَ الْحَيْضِ فِرْصَةً مِنْ مِسْكٍ فِي مَوْضِعِ الدَّمِ ‏

chapters.title: The ghusl and the prayer of a woman who is suffering prolonged vaginal bleeding (istihadah)
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمُسْتَحَاضَةِ وَغُسْلِهَا وَصَلاَتِهَا ‏

chapters.title: A menstruating woman is obliged to make up missed fasts but not prayers
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ قَضَاءِ الصَّوْمِ عَلَى الْحَائِضِ دُونَ الصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: Covering oneself with a garment and the like while performing ghusl
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَسَتُّرِ الْمُغْتَسِلِ بِثَوْبٍ وَنَحْوِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of looking at `awrah
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْرِيمِ النَّظَرِ إِلَى الْعَوْرَاتِ ‏

chapters.title: It is permissible to bathe naked when one is alone
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ الاِغْتِسَالِ عُرْيَانًا فِي الْخَلْوَةِ ‏

chapters.title: Taking care to conceal one’s `awrah
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِعْتِنَاءِ بِحِفْظِ الْعَوْرَةِ

chapters.title: Screening oneself when urinating
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُسْتَتَرُ بِهِ لِقَضَاءِ الْحَاجَةِ ‏

chapters.title: At the beginning of Islam, intercourse did not necessitate ghusl unless semen was emitted, then that was abrogated and ghusl becomes obligatory for intercourse
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِنَّمَا الْمَاءُ مِنَ الْمَاءِ ‏

chapters.title: Abrogation of “water is for water”, and that it is obligatory to perform ghusl when the two circumcised parts meet
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَسْخِ ‏"‏ الْمَاءُ مِنَ الْمَاءِ ‏"‏ ‏وَوُجُوبِ الْغُسْلِ بِالْتِقَاءِ الْخِتَانَيْنِ ‏

chapters.title: Performing wudu’ after eating something that has been touched by fire
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوُضُوءِ مِمَّا مَسَّتِ النَّارُ ‏

chapters.title: Abrogation of wudu’ for that which has been touched by fire
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَسْخِ ‏"‏ الْوُضُوءِ مِمَّا مَسَّتِ النَّارُ ‏"‏ ‏

chapters.title: (Performing) wudu’ after eating camel meat
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْوُضُوءِ مِنْ لُحُومِ الإِبِلِ ‏

chapters.title: Evidence that if a person is certain that he is in a state of purity, then he doubts whether he has committed hadith (broken his wudu’), then he prays with his purity like that
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ مَنْ تَيَقَّنَ الطَّهَارَةَ ثُمَّ شَكَّ فِي الْحَدَثِ فَلَهُ أَنْ يُصَلِّيَ بِطَهَارَتِهِ تِلْكَ

chapters.title: Hides of dead animals are purified by tanning
chapters.title_arabic: باب طَهَارَةِ جُلُودِ الْمَيْتَةِ بِالدِّبَاغِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Tayammum
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّيَمُّمِ ‏

chapters.title: Evidence that the Muslim does not become impure
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ الْمُسْلِمَ لاَ يَنْجُسُ ‏‏

chapters.title: Remembering Allah, the Most High, when one is sexually impure, and at other times
chapters.title_arabic: باب ذِكْرِ اللَّهِ تَعَالَى فِي حَالِ الْجَنَابَةِ وَغَيْرِهَا ‏

chapters.title: It is permissible for one who has broken his wudu’ to eat, and there is nothing disliked about doing so, and wudu’ need not be done immediately
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ أَكْلِ الْمُحْدِثِ الطَّعَامَ وَأَنَّهُ لاَ كَرَاهَةَ فِي ذَلِكَ وَأَنَّ الْوُضُوءَ لَيْسَ عَلَى الْفَوْرِ

chapters.title: What should be said when entering the area in which one relieves himself
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ إِذَا أَرَادَ دُخُولَ الْخَلاَءِ ‏

chapters.title: Evidence that sleeping while sitting does not invalidate wudu’
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ عَلَى أَنَّ نَوْمَ الْجَالِسِ لاَ يَنْقُضُ الْوُضُوءَ ‏

chapters.title: The Beginning Of The Adhan
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَدْءِ الأَذَانِ

chapters.title: The Command to say the phrases of the Adhan twice and the phrases of the iqamah once, except the phrase, "Prayer is about to begin," which is to be said twice
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَمْرِ بِشَفْعِ الأَذَانِ وَإِيتَارِ الإِقَامَةِ ‏

chapters.title: Description Of The Adhan
chapters.title_arabic: باب صِفَةِ الأَذَانِ ‏

chapters.title: It Is Recommended To Have Two Mu'adhdhin In A Single Masjid
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ اتِّخَاذِ مُؤَذِّنَيْنِ لِلْمَسْجِدِ الْوَاحِدِ ‏‏

chapters.title: It Is Permissible For A Blind Man To Call The Adhan So long As There Is A Man With Him Who Sees
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ أَذَانِ الأَعْمَى إِذَا كَانَ مَعَهُ بَصِيرٌ ‏

chapters.title: Refraining From Attacking People In Dar Al-Kufr (Non-Muslim Lands) If The Adhan Is Heard Among Them
chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِمْسَاكِ عَنِ الإِغَارَةِ، عَلَى قَوْمٍ فِي دَارِ الْكُفْرِ إِذَا سُمِعَ فِيهِمُ الأَذَانُ ‏

chapters.title: It Is Recommended For The One Who Hears The Mu'adhdhin To Repeat His Words, Then To Send Salat Upon The Prophet (saws) And Ask Allah To Grant Him Al-Wasilah
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ الْقَوْلِ مِثْلَ قَوْلِ الْمُؤَذِّنِ لِمَنْ سَمِعَهُ ثُمَّ يُصَلِّي عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ ثُمَّ يَسْأَلُ اللَّهَ لَهُ الْوَسِيلَةَ

chapters.title: The Virtue Of the Adhan, And The Shaitan Flees When He Hears It
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الأَذَانِ وَهَرَبِ الشَّيْطَانِ عِنْدَ سَمَاعِهِ ‏

chapters.title: It Is Recommended To Raise The Hands Level With The Shoulders When Saying The Opening Takbir, When Bowing And When Rising From Bowing. But That Is Not To Be Done When Rising From The Prostration
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ رَفْعِ الْيَدَيْنِ حَذْوَ الْمَنْكِبَيْنِ مَعَ تَكْبِيرَةِ الإِحْرَامِ وَالرُّكُوعِ وَفِي الرَّفْعِ مِنَ الرُّكُوعِ وَأَنَّهُ لاَ يَفْعَلُهُ إِذَا رَفَعَ مِنَ السُّجُودِ

chapters.title: Affirming The Takbir For Every Movement Up Or Down In The Prayer, Except When Rising From Ruku' When One Should Say: Sami'aAllahu Liman Hamidah (Allah Hears Those Who Praise Him)
chapters.title_arabic: باب إِثْبَاتِ التَّكْبِيرِ فِي كُلِّ خَفْضٍ وَرَفْعٍ فِي الصَّلاَةِ إِلاَّ رَفْعَهُ مِنَ الرُّكُوعِ فَيَقُولُ فِيهِ سَمِعَ اللَّهُ لِمَنْ حَمِدَهُ

chapters.title: It Is Obligatory To Recite Al-Fatihah In Every Rak'ah; If A Person Cannot Recite Al-Fatihah Or Cannot Learn It, Then He Should Recite Whatever Else He Can Manage
chapters.title_arabic: باب وُجُوبِ قِرَاءَةِ الْفَاتِحَةِ فِي كُلِّ رَكْعَةٍ وَإِنَّهُ إِذَا لَمْ يُحْسِنِ الْفَاتِحَةَ وَلاَ أَمْكَنَهُ تَعَلُّمُهَا قَرَأَ مَا تَيَسَّرَ لَهُ مِنْ غَيْرِهَا

chapters.title: Prohibiting The Follower From Reciting Aloud Behind An Imam
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَهْىِ الْمَأْمُومِ عَنْ جَهْرِهِ، بِالْقِرَاءَةِ خَلْفَ إِمَامِهِ ‏

chapters.title: The Proof Of Those Who Say That The Basmalah Should Not Be Recited Aloud
chapters.title_arabic: باب حُجَّةِ مَنْ قَالَ لاَ يَجْهَرُ بِالْبَسْمَلَةِ ‏

chapters.title: The Proof Of Those Who Say That The Bismillah Is A Verse At The Beginning Of Every Surah, Except Bara'ah (At-Tawbah)
chapters.title_arabic: باب حُجَّةِ مَنْ قَالَ الْبَسْمَلَةُ آيَةٌ مِنْ أَوَّلِ كُلِّ سُورَةٍ سِوَى بَرَاءَةَ‏‏

chapters.title: The Placing Of The Right Hand Over The Left Hand After The First Takbir In Prayer (takbir-i-tahrima) Below The Chest And Above The Navel And Then Placing Them Apposite The Shoulders In Prostration
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَضْعِ يَدِهِ الْيُمْنَى عَلَى الْيُسْرَى بَعْدَ تَكْبِيرَةِ الإِحْرَامِ تَحْتَ صَدْرِهِ فَوْقَ سُرَّتِهِ وَوَضْعِهِمَا فِي السُّجُودِ عَلَى الأَرْضِ حَذْوَ مَنْكِبَيْهِ

chapters.title: The Tashah-hud In The Prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّشَهُّدِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: Sending Salat Upon The Prophet (saws) After The Tashah-hud
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم بَعْدَ التَّشَهُّدِ ‏

chapters.title: Saying "Sami'a Allahu liman Hamidah," Rabbana wa lakal-hamd," and "Amin"
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّسْمِيعِ وَالتَّحْمِيدِ وَالتَّأْمِينِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Following The Imam
chapters.title_arabic: باب ائْتِمَامِ الْمَأْمُومِ بِالإِمَامِ ‏

chapters.title: The Prohibition Of Preceding The Imam In Saying The Takbir Or Anything Else
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنْ مُبَادَرَةِ الإِمَامِ، بِالتَّكْبِيرِ وَغَيْرِهِ ‏

chapters.title: If The Imam Experiences An Excuse, From Illness, Or Travelling, Etc. He May Appoint Someone Else To Lead The People In Prayer; The One Who Offers Prayer Behind The Imam Sitting Because He Is Unable To Stand Must Stand If He Is Able To Do So; And The Abrogation Of Sitting Behind A Sitting Imam For Those Who Are Able To Stand
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِخْلاَفِ الإِمَامِ إِذَا عَرَضَ لَهُ عُذْرٌ مِنْ مَرَضٍ وَسَفَرٍ وَغَيْرِهِمَا مَنْ يُصَلِّي بِالنَّاسِ وَأَنَّ مَنْ صَلَّى خَلْفَ إِمَامٍ جَالِسٍ لِعَجْزِهِ عَنِ الْقِيَامِ لَزِمَهُ الْقِيَامُ إِذَا قَدَرَ عَلَيْهِ وَنَسْخِ الْقُعُودِ خَلْفَ الْقَاعِدِ فِي حَقِّ مَنْ قَدَرَ عَلَى الْقِيَامِ

chapters.title: Men Saying The Tasbih And Women Clapping If They Notice Anything During The Prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَسْبِيحِ الرَّجُلِ وَتَصْفِيقِ الْمَرْأَةِ إِذَا نَابَهُمَا شَىْءٌ فِي الصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: The Command To Perform The Prayer Properly, To Complete It, And To Have Khushu' In It
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَمْرِ بِتَحْسِينِ الصَّلاَةِ وَإِتْمَامِهَا وَالْخُشُوعِ فِيهَا ‏

chapters.title: The Prohibition Of Preceding The Imam While Bowing, Prostrating And So On
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنْ سَبْقِ الإِمَامِ، بِرُكُوعٍ أَوْ سُجُودٍ وَنَحْوِهِمَا ‏

chapters.title: The Prohibition On Lifting Ones Gaze To The Heavens When in Salat

chapters.title: The Command To Be Calm During The Prayer And The Prohibition Of Gesturing With One's Hand And Raising It When Saying The Salam; And Completing The First Rows, Aligning In Them, And The Command To Come Together
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَمْرِ بَالسُّكُونِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ وَالنَّهْيِ عَنِ الإِشَارَةِ بِالْيَدِ وَرَفْعِهَا عِنْدَ السَّلاَمِ وَإِتْمَامِ الصُّفُوفِ الأُوَلِ وَالتَّرَاصِّ فِيهَا وَالأَمْرِ بِالاِجْتِمَاعِ

chapters.title: Straightening The Rows; The Virtue Of The Front Row And Then The Next; Competing With One Another For The Front Row; The People Of Virtue Should Take Precedence And Be Closest To The Imam
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَسْوِيَةِ الصُّفُوفِ وَإِقَامَتِهَا وَفَضْلِ الأَوَّلِ فَالأَوَّلِ مِنْهَا وَالاِزْدِحَامِ عَلَى الصَّفِّ الأَوَّلِ وَالْمُسَابَقَةِ إِلَيْهَا وَتَقْدِيمِ أُولِي الْفَضْلِ وَتَقْرِيبِهِمْ مِنَ الإِمَامِ

chapters.title_arabic: باب أَمْرِ النِّسَاءِ الْمُصَلِّيَاتِ وَرَاءَ الرِّجَالِ أَنْ لاَ يَرْفَعْنَ رُءُوسَهُنَّ مِنَ السُّجُودِ حَتَّى يَرْفَعَ الرِّجَالُ

chapters.title: Women Going Out To The Masjid So Long As No Fitnah Results From That; and They Should Not Go Out Wearing Perfume
chapters.title_arabic: باب خُرُوجِ النِّسَاءِ إِلَى الْمَسَاجِدِ إِذَا لَمْ يَتَرَتَّبْ عَلَيْهِ فِتْنَةٌ وَأَنَّهَا لاَ تَخْرُجُ مُطَيَّبَةً

chapters.title: Moderation When Reciting Qur'an In A Prayer When It Is To Be Recited Out Loud, And Making it Neither Too Loud Nor Too Soft, And When There Is The Fear Of Negative Consequences If It Is Recited Out Loud
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّوَسُّطِ فِي الْقِرَاءَةِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ الْجَهْرِيَّةِ بَيْنَ الْجَهْرِ وَالإِسْرَارِ إِذَا خَافَ مِنَ الْجَهْرِ مَفْسَدَةً

chapters.title: The Recitation For Zuhr And 'Asr

chapters.title: The Command To The Imam To Make The Prayer Brief But Complete
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَمْرِ الأَئِمَّةِ بِتَخْفِيفِ الصَّلاَةِ فِي تَمَامٍ ‏

chapters.title_arabic: باب اعْتِدَالِ أَرْكَانِ الصَّلاَةِ وَتَخْفِيفِهَا فِي تَمَامٍ ‏

chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ إِذَا رَفَعَ رَأْسَهُ مِنَ الرُّكُوعِ ‏

chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِعْتِدَالِ فِي السُّجُودِ وَوَضْعِ الْكَفَّيْنِ عَلَى الأَرْضِ وَرَفْعِ الْمِرْفَقَيْنِ عَنِ الْجَنْبَيْنِ وَرَفْعِ الْبَطْنِ عَنِ الْفَخِذَيْنِ فِي السُّجُودِ

chapters.title: The Description Of The Prayer, With What It Begins And Ends. The Description Of Bowing And Moderation Therein, And Of Prostration And Moderation Therein. Tashah-hud After Each Two Rak'ah Of Four Rak'ah Prayers. Description Ol Sitting Between The Two Prostrations, And In The First Tashah-hud
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَجْمَعُ صِفَةَ الصَّلاَةِ وَمَا يُفْتَتَحُ بِهِ وَيُخْتَمُ بِهِ وَصِفَةَ الرُّكُوعِ وَالاِعْتِدَالِ مِنْهُ وَالسُّجُودِ وَالاِعْتِدَالِ مِنْهُ وَالتَّشَهُّدِ بَعْدَ كُلِّ رَكْعَتَيْنِ مِنَ الرُّبَاعِيَّةِ وَصِفَةَ الْجُلُوسِ بَيْنَ السَّجْدَتَيْنِ وَفِي التَّشَهُّدِ الأَوَّلِ

chapters.title: The Sutrah (Screen) For One Who Is Praying, And The Recommendation To Pray Facing A Sutrah. The Ruling On Passing In Front Of One Who Is Praying, And Preventing One Who Wants To Pass In Front. It Is Permissible To Lie Down In Front Of One Who Is Praying, Praying Towards One's Mount. The Command To Stand Close To The Sutrah. The Height Of The Suirah, And Related Matters
chapters.title_arabic: باب سُتْرَةِ الْمُصَلِّي ‏

chapters.title: Preventing One Who Wants To Pass In Front Of A Praying Person
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْعِ الْمَارِّ بَيْنَ يَدَىِ الْمُصَلِّي ‏

chapters.title: The Praying Person Standing Close To The Sutrah
chapters.title_arabic: باب دُنُوِّ الْمُصَلِّي مِنَ السُّتْرَةِ ‏

chapters.title_arabic: باب قَدْرِ مَا يَسْتُرُ الْمُصَلِّيَ ‏‏

chapters.title: Lying In Front Of One Who Is Praying
chapters.title_arabic: باب الاِعْتِرَاضِ بَيْنَ يَدَىِ الْمُصَلِّي ‏

chapters.title: Praying In A Single Garment, And How It Should Be Worn
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي ثَوْبٍ وَاحِدٍ وَصِفَةِ لُبْسِهِ ‏

chapters.title: The construction of the Masjid of the Prophet (saws)
chapters.title_arabic: باب ابْتِنَاءِ مَسْجِدِ النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم ‏

chapters.title: Changing the qiblah from Al-Quds (Jerusalem) to the Ka`bah
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْوِيلِ الْقِبْلَةِ مِنَ الْقُدْسِ إِلَى الْكَعْبَةِ ‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of building masajid over graves and placing images therein; and the prohibition of taking graves as masajid
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْيِ عَنْ بِنَاءِ الْمَسَاجِدِ عَلَى الْقُبُورِ وَاتِّخَاذِ الصُّوَرِ فِيهَا وَالنَّهْيِ عَنِ اتِّخَاذِ الْقُبُورِ مَسَاجِدَ

chapters.title: The virtue of building masajid and encouragement to do so
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ بِنَاءِ الْمَسَاجِدِ وَالْحَثِّ عَلَيْهَا ‏

chapters.title: The recommendation to place the hands on the knees when bowing, and the abrogation of tatbiq
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّدْبِ إِلَى وَضْعِ الأَيْدِي عَلَى الرُّكَبِ فِي الرُّكُوعِ وَنَسْخِ التَّطْبِيقِ ‏

chapters.title: The permissibility of squatting on the heels
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ الإِقْعَاءِ عَلَى الْعَقِبَيْنِ ‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of speaking during the prayer, and the abrogation of its former permissibility
chapters.title_arabic: باب تَحْرِيمِ الْكَلاَمِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ وَنَسْخِ مَا كَانَ مِنْ إِبَاحَتِهِ ‏

chapters.title: The permissibility of cursing the Shaitan during prayer, and seeking refuge with Allah from him; and the permissibility of doing a few actions while in salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ لَعْنِ الشَّيْطَانِ فِي أَثْنَاءِ الصَّلاَةِ وَالتَّعَوُّذِ مِنْهُ وَجَوَازِ الْعَمَلِ الْقَلِيلِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ

chapters.title: The permissibility of carrying children during prayer, and their garments are regarded as being pure until it is realized that they are impure. Few actions will not invalidate the prayer, and the same applies if several such actions are done but are done separately
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ حَمْلِ الصِّبْيَانِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: The permissibility of taking one or two steps while praying, and that is not disliked if done for a reason. The permissibility of the Imam praying in a place that is higher than the people praying behind him, if that is done for a reason, such as teaching them how to offer prayers, and other than that.
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ الْخُطْوَةِ وَالْخُطْوَتَيْنِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: It is disliked to put the hands on the waist during the prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهَةِ الاِخْتِصَارِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: It is disliked to smooth the pebbles or make the dirt level during salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهَةِ مَسْحِ الْحَصَى وَتَسْوِيَةِ التُّرَابِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of spitting in the masjid, during prayer and at other times. The prohibition of a praying person spitting in front of him or to his right
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنِ الْبُصَاقِ، فِي الْمَسْجِدِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ وَغَيْرِهَا ‏‏

chapters.title: The permissibility of offering salat while wearing shoes
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ الصَّلاَةِ فِي النَّعْلَيْنِ ‏

chapters.title: It is disliked to offer salat in a garment with markings
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهَةِ الصَّلاَةِ فِي ثَوْبٍ لَهُ أَعْلاَمٌ‏

chapters.title: It is disliked to offer salat in the presence of food that one wants to eat. It is disliked to offer salat while restraining the urge to relieve oneself, and so on
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهَةِ الصَّلاَةِ بِحَضْرَةِ الطَّعَامِ الَّذِي يُرِيدُ أَكْلَهُ فِي الْحَالِ وَكَرَاهَةِ الصَّلاَةِ مَعَ مُدَافَعَةِ الأَخْبَثَيْنِ

chapters.title: Prohibiting one who has eaten garlic, onions, or leeks, and other things that have an offensive odor from coming to the masjid, until that smell has gone away, and such a person should be expelled from the masjid
chapters.title_arabic: باب نَهْىِ مَنْ أَكَلَ ثُومًا أَوْ بَصَلاً أَوْ كُرَّاثًا أَوْ نَحْوَهَا عَنْ حُضُورِ الْمَسْجِدِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of making lost property announcements in the masjid, and what should be said by one who hears a person making such an announcement
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنْ نَشْدِ الضَّالَّةِ، فِي الْمَسْجدِ وَمَا يَقُولُهُ مَنْ سَمِعَ النَّاشِدَ ‏

chapters.title: As-Sahw (forgetfulness) in prayer and prostrating to compensate for it
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّهْوِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ وَالسُّجُودِ لَهُ ‏

chapters.title: The prostration of recitation
chapters.title_arabic: باب سُجُودِ التِّلاَوَةِ ‏

chapters.title: The description of the sitting during the prayer, and how the hands are to be placed on the thighs
chapters.title_arabic: باب صِفَةِ الْجُلُوسِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ وَكَيْفِيَّةِ وَضْعِ الْيَدَيْنِ عَلَى الْفَخِذَيْنِ ‏

chapters.title: The salam to exit the prayer when one has finished and how it is done
chapters.title_arabic: باب السَّلاَمِ لِلتَّحْلِيلِ مِنَ الصَّلاَةِ عِنْدَ فَرَاغِهَا وَكَيْفِيَّتِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The remembrance after the prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الذِّكْرِ بَعْدَ الصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: It is recommended to seek refuge with Allah from the punishment of the grave, the punishment of hell, the trials of life and death, the tribulation of the Dajjal and from sin and debt between the tashah-hud and the taslim
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ التَّعَوُّذِ مِنْ عَذَابِ الْقَبْرِ ‏

chapters.title: From what refuge is to be sought when in salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُسْتَعَاذُ مِنْهُ فِي الصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: It is recommended to recite statements of remembrance after the prayer, and how that is to be done
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ الذِّكْرِ بَعْدَ الصَّلاَةِ وَبَيَان صِفَتِهِ ‏

chapters.title: What is to be said between the opening takbir and the recitation of the Qur’an
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُقَالُ بَيْنَ تَكْبِيرَةِ الإِحْرَامِ وَالْقِرَاءَةِ

chapters.title: It is recommended to come to prayers in a tranquil and dignified manner, and it is forbidden to come in a hasty manner
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ إِتْيَانِ الصَّلاَةِ بِوَقَارٍ وَسَكِينَةٍ وَالنَّهْىِ عَنْ إِتْيَانِهَا سَعْيًا ‏‏

chapters.title: When should the people stand up to pray?
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَتَى يَقُومُ النَّاسُ لِلصَّلاَةِ ‏

chapters.title: The one who catches up with a rak`ah of the prayer, he has caught up with that prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَدْرَكَ رَكْعَةً مِنَ الصَّلاَةِ فَقَدْ أَدْرَكَ تِلْكَ الصَّلاَةَ ‏

chapters.title: The times of the five prayers
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَوْقَاتِ الصَّلَوَاتِ الْخَمْسِ ‏

chapters.title: The recommendation to delay Zuhr (until it has cooled down) in severe heat for one who finds it too hot in route to join the congregation
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ الإِبْرَادِ بِالظُّهْرِ فِي شِدَّةِ الْحَرِّ لِمَنْ يَمْضِي إِلَى جَمَاعَةٍ وَيَنَالُهُ الْحَرُّ فِي طَرِيقِهِ

chapters.title: It is recommended to pray Zuhr at the beginning of its time when there is no intense heat
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ تَقْدِيمِ الظُّهْرِ فِي أَوَّلِ الْوَقْتِ فِي غَيْرِ شِدَّةِ الْحَرِّ ‏‏

chapters.title: It is recommended to pray `Asr early
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ التَّبْكِيرِ بِالْعَصْرِ

chapters.title: Stern warning against missing the `Asr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّغْلِيظِ فِي تَفْوِيتِ صَلاَةِ الْعَصْرِ ‏

chapters.title: The evidence for those who say that “the middle prayer” is the `Asr prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدَّلِيلِ لِمَنْ قَالَ الصَّلاَةُ الْوُسْطَى هِيَ صَلاَةُ الْعَصْرِ ‏

chapters.title: The virtue of the Subh and the `Asr prayers, and of maintaining them
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ صَلاَتَىِ الصُّبْحِ وَالْعَصْرِ وَالْمُحَافَظَةِ عَلَيْهِمَا ‏

chapters.title: The beginning of the time for Maghrib when the sun sets
chapters.title_arabic: باب بَيَانِ أَنَّ أَوَّلَ وَقْتِ الْمَغْرِبِ عِنْدَ غُرُوبِ الشَّمْسِ ‏

chapters.title: The time of `Isha and delaying it
chapters.title_arabic: باب وَقْتِ الْعِشَاءِ وَتَأْخِيرِهَا ‏

chapters.title: It is recommended to pray Subh early, at the beginning of its time, when it is still dark; and the length of recitation therein
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ التَّبْكِيرِ بِالصُّبْحِ فِي أَوَّلِ وَقْتِهَا وَهُوَ التَّغْلِيسُ وَبَيَانِ قَدْرِ الْقِرَاءَةِ فِيهَا

chapters.title: It is disliked to delay the prayers beyond their proper times, and what a person should do if the Imam delays the prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهِيَةِ تَأْخِيرِ الصَّلاَةِ عَنْ وَقْتِهَا الْمُخْتَارِ وَمَا يَفْعَلُهُ الْمَأْمُومُ إِذَا أَخَّرَهَا الإِمَامُ

chapters.title: The virtue of prayer in congregation, and clarifying the stern warning against staying away from it, and that it is fard kifayah
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ صَلاَةِ الجَمَاعَةِ وَبَيَانِ التَّشْدِيدِ فِي التَّخَلُّفِ عَنْهَا ‏

chapters.title: It is obligatory for the one who hears the call to prayer to come to the masjid
chapters.title_arabic: باب يَجِبُ إِتْيَانُ الْمَسْجِدِ عَلَى مَنْ سَمِعَ النِّدَاءَ ‏

chapters.title: Prayer in congregation is one of the sunnah of guidance
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةُ الْجَمَاعَةِ مِنْ سُنَنِ الْهُدَى ‏

chapters.title: The prohibition of leaving the masjid once the Mu’adhdhin has called the Adhan
chapters.title_arabic: باب النَّهْىِ عَنِ الْخُرُوجِ، مِنَ الْمَسْجِدِ إِذَا أَذَّنَ الْمُؤَذِّنُ ‏

chapters.title: The virtue of praying `Isha and Subh in congregation
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ صَلاَةِ الْعِشَاءِ وَالصُّبْحِ فِي جَمَاعَةٍ ‏‏

chapters.title: Concession allowing one to not attend the congregation if there is an excuse
chapters.title_arabic: باب الرُّخْصَةِ فِي التَّخَلُّفِ عَنِ الْجَمَاعَةِ، بِعُذْرٍ ‏

chapters.title: It is permissible to offer voluntary prayers in congregation, and to pray on hasir (palm-fiber mats), khumrah (small mats), cloth, and other pure things
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ الْجَمَاعَةِ فِي النَّافِلَةِ وَالصَّلاَةِ عَلَى حَصِيرٍ وَخُمْرَةٍ وَثَوْبٍ وَغَيْرِهَا مِنَ الطَّاهِرَاتِ

chapters.title: The virtue of offering the obligatory prayers in congregation, the virtue of waiting for prayer and taking many steps towards the masjid, the virtue of walking to the masjid
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ صَلاَةِ الْجَمَاعَةِ وَانْتِظَارِ الصَّلاَةِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The virtue of taking many steps to the masjid
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ كَثْرَةِ الْخُطَا إِلَى الْمَسَاجِدِ ‏

chapters.title: Walking to prayer erases sins and raises one in status
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْمَشْىُ إِلَى الصَّلاَةِ تُمْحَى بِهِ الْخَطَايَا وَتُرْفَعُ بِهِ الدَّرَجَاتُ ‏

chapters.title: The virtue of sitting in one’s prayer place after Subh, and the virtue of the masajid
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الْجُلُوسِ فِي مُصَلاَّهُ بَعْدَ الصُّبْحِ وَفَضْلِ الْمَسَاجِدِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Who is more entitled to lead the prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ أَحَقُّ بِالإِمَامَةِ

chapters.title: It is recommended to say qunut in all prayers if a calamity befalls the Muslims – and refuge is sought from Allah (regarding that). It is recommended to say qunut in Subh at all times. And the clarification that it is to be said after raising the head from bowing in the final rak`ah, and it is recommended to say it out loud
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ الْقُنُوتِ فِي جَمِيعِ الصَّلاَةِ إِذَا نزَلَتْ بِالْمُسْلِمِينَ نَازِلَةٌ ‏‏

chapters.title: Making up a missed prayer. And it is recommended to hasten to make it up
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَضَاءِ الصَّلاَةِ الْفَائِتَةِ وَاسْتِحْبَابِ تَعْجِيلِ قَضَائِهَا ‏‏

chapters.title: The travelers’ prayer and shortening it
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ الْمُسَافِرِينَ وَقَصْرِهَا ‏‏

chapters.title: Shortening the prayer in Mina
chapters.title_arabic: باب قَصْرِ الصَّلاَةِ بِمِنًى ‏‏

chapters.title: Praying in dwellings when it is raining
chapters.title_arabic: باب الصَّلاَةِ فِي الرحَالِ فِي الْمَطَرِ ‏‏

chapters.title: It is permissible to offer voluntary prayers atop one’s mount when travelling, no matter what direction it is facing
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ صَلاَةِ النَّافِلَةِ عَلَى الدَّابَّةِ فِي السَّفَرِ حَيْثُ تَوَجَّهَتْ ‏‏

chapters.title: It is permissible to combine two prayers when traveling
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ الْجَمْعِ بَيْنَ الصَّلاَتَيْنِ فِي السَّفَرِ ‏

chapters.title: Joining two prayers when not traveling
chapters.title_arabic: باب الْجَمْعِ بَيْنَ الصَّلاَتَيْنِ فِي الْحَضَرِ ‏

chapters.title: It is permissible to leave to the right or left after finishing the prayer
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَاز الاِنْصِرَافِ مِنَ الصَّلاَةِ عَنِ الْيَمِينِ، وَالشِّمَالِ‏

chapters.title: It is recommended to stand to the right of the Imam
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ يَمِينِ الإِمَامِ ‏‏

chapters.title: It is disliked to start a voluntary prayer after the Mu’adhdhin has started to say Iqamah for prayer, whether that is a regular sunnah, such as the sunnah of Subh or Zuhr, or anything else, and regardless of whether he knows that he will catch up with the rak`ah with the Imam or not
chapters.title_arabic: باب كَرَاهَةِ الشُّرُوعِ فِي نَافِلَةٍ بَعْدَ شُرُوعِ الْمُؤَذِّنِ ‏

chapters.title: What to say when entering the masjid
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يَقُولُ إِذَا دَخَلَ الْمَسْجِدَ ‏‏

chapters.title: It is recommended to greet the masjid by praying two rak`ah, and it is disliked to sit before praying these two rak`ah, and this is prescribed at all times
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ تَحِيَّةِ الْمَسْجِدِ بِرَكْعَتَيْنِ وَكَرَاهَةِ الْجُلُوسِ قَبْلَ صَلاَتِهِمَا وَأَنَّهَا مَشْرُوعَةٌ فِي جَمِيعِ الأَوْقَاتِ

chapters.title: It is recommended to pray two rak`ah in the masjid for one who has come from a journey, when he first arrives
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ الرَّكْعَتَيْنِ فِي الْمَسْجِدِ لِمَنْ قَدِمَ مِنْ سَفَرٍ أَوَّلَ قُدُومِهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: It is recommended to pray Duha, the least of which is two rak`ah, the best of which is eight, and the average of which is four or six, and encouragement to do so regularly
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ صَلاَةِ الضُّحَى وَأَنَّ أَقَلَّهَا رَكْعَتَانِ وَأَكْمَلَهَا ثَمَانِ رَكَعَاتٍ وَأَوْسَطَهَا أَرْبَعُ رَكَعَاتٍ أَوْ سِتٌّ وَالْحَثِّ عَلَى الْمُحَافَظَةِ عَلَيْهَا

chapters.title: It is recommended to pray two rak`ah for the sunnah of Fajr. And encouragement to pray them regularly, and to make them brief, and to persist in offering them, and clarifying what is recommended to recite therein
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ رَكْعَتَيْ سُنَّةِ الْفَجْرِ وَالْحَثِّ عَلَيْهِمَا وَتَخْفِيفِهِمَا وَالْمُحَافَظَةِ عَلَيْهِمَا وَبَيَانِ مَا يُسْتَحَبُّ أَنْ يُقْرَأَ فِيهِمَا

chapters.title: The virtue of the regular sunnah prayers before and after the obligatory prayers, and their numbers
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ السُّنَنِ الرَّاتِبَةِ قَبْلَ الْفَرَائِضِ وَبَعْدَهُنَّ وَبَيَانِ عَدَدِهِنَّ

chapters.title: It is permissible to offer voluntary prayers standing or sitting, and to stand and sit in the same rak`ah
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَوَازِ النَّافِلَةِ قَائِمًا وَقَاعِدًا وَفِعْلِ بَعْضِ الرَّكْعَةِ قَائِمًا وَبَعْضِهَا قَاعِدًا ‏

chapters.title: Night prayers and the number of rak`ah offered by the Prophet (saws) at night, and that Witr is one rak`ah, and a one-rak`ah prayer is correct
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةِ اللَّيْلِ وَعَدَدِ رَكَعَاتِ النَّبِيِّ صَلَّى اللَّهُ عَلَيْهِ وَسَلَّمَ فِي اللَّيْلِ وَأَنَّ الْوِتْرَ رَكْعَةٌ وَأَنَّ الرَّكْعَةَ صَلاَةٌ صَحِيحَةٌ

chapters.title: Night prayer, and the one who sleeps and misses it or is sick
chapters.title_arabic: باب جَامِعِ صَلاَةِ اللَّيْلِ وَمَنْ نَامَ عَنْهُ أَوْ مَرِضَ ‏

chapters.title: Salat al-Awwabin (the prayer of the penitent) is when the young camels feel the heat of the hot sand
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةُ الأَوَّابِينَ حِينَ تَرْمَضُ الْفِصَالُ ‏

chapters.title: The night prayers are two by two, and Witr is one rakah at the end of the night
chapters.title_arabic: باب صَلاَةُ اللَّيْلِ مَثْنَى مَثْنَى وَالْوِتْرُ رَكْعَةٌ مِنْ آخِرِ اللَّيْلِ ‏

chapters.title: One who fears that he will not get up at the end of the night, then he should pray Witr at the beginning of the night
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ خَافَ أَنْ لاَ يَقُومَ مِنْ آخِرِ اللَّيْلِ فَلْيُوتِرْ أَوَّلَهُ ‏

chapters.title: The best prayer is that in which one stands for a long time (tuwlul-qunut)
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَفْضَلُ الصَّلاَةِ طُولُ الْقُنُوتِ ‏

chapters.title: In the night there is an hour when supplications are answered
chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي اللَّيْلِ سَاعَةٌ مُسْتَجَابٌ فِيهَا الدُّعَاءُ ‏

chapters.title: Encouragement to supplicate and recite statements of remembrance at the end of the night, and the response to that
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّرْغِيبِ فِي الدُّعَاءِ وَالذِّكْرِ فِي آخِرِ اللَّيْلِ وَالإِجَابَةِ فِيهِ ‏

chapters.title: Encouragement to pray qiyam during Ramadan, which is Tarawih
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّرْغِيبِ فِي قِيَامِ رَمَضَانَ وَهُوَ التَّرَاوِيحُ ‏

chapters.title: The prayer and the supplication of the Prophet (saws) at night
chapters.title_arabic: باب الدُّعَاءِ فِي صَلاَةِ اللَّيْلِ وَقِيَامِهِ ‏

chapters.title: It is recommended to recite for a long time in the night prayers
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ تَطْوِيلِ الْقِرَاءَةِ فِي صَلاَةِ اللَّيْلِ ‏‏

chapters.title: Encouragement to pray at night even if it is little
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا رُوِيَ فِيمَنْ، نَامَ اللَّيْلَ أَجْمَعَ حَتَّى أَصْبَحَ ‏

chapters.title: It is recommended to offer voluntary prayers in one’s house although it is permissible to offer them in the masjid
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ صَلاَةِ النَّافِلَةِ فِي بَيْتِهِ وَجَوَازِهَا فِي الْمَسْجِدِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The virtue of a deed that is done persistently, whether it be Qiyam al-Lail or anything else. The command to be moderate in worship, which means adopting what one can persist in. The command to the one who gets tired or weary when praying to stop until that feeling passes
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضِيلَةِ الْعَمَلِ الدَّائِمِ مِنْ قِيَامِ اللَّيْلِ وَغَيْرِهِ ‏

chapters.title: The command to one who becomes sleepy while praying, or who starts to falter in his recitation of the Qur’an or statements of remembrance, to lie down or sit down until that goes away
chapters.title_arabic: باب أَمْرِ مَنْ نَعَسَ فِي صَلاَتِهِ أَوِ اسْتَعْجَمَ عَلَيْهِ الْقُرْآنُ أَوِ الذِّكْرُ بِأَنْ يَرْقُدَ أَوْ يَقْعُدَ حَتَّى يَذْهَبَ عَنْهُ ذَلِكَ

chapters.title: The book of the virtues of the Qur’an etc
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضَائِلِ الْقُرْآنِ وَمَا يَتَعَلَّقُ بِهِ

chapters.title: The command to keep refreshing one’s knowledge of the Qur’an and that it is disliked to say I have forgotten such-and-such a verse, but it is permissible to say I have been caused to forget
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَمْرِ بِتَعَهُّدِ الْقُرْآنِ وَكَرَاهَةِ قَوْلِ نَسِيتُ آيَةَ كَذَا. وَجَوَازِ قَوْلِ أُنْسِيتُهَا

chapters.title: It is recommended to make one’s voice beautiful when reciting Qur’an
chapters.title_arabic: باب اسْتِحْبَابِ تَحْسِينِ الصَّوْتِ بِالْقُرْآنِ ‏

chapters.title: The descent of tranquility (sakinah) when the Qur’an is recited
chapters.title_arabic: باب نُزُولِ السَّكِينَةِ لِقِرَاءَةِ الْقُرْآنِ ‏

chapters.title: The virtue of the one who memorizes the Qur’an

chapters.title: It is recommended to recite the Qur’an to people of virtue who are skilled in its recitation, even if the reciter is better than the one to whom it is recited

chapters.title: The virtue of listening to the Qur’an, asking one who has memorized it to recite so that one may listen, weeping when reciting, and pondering the meanings
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ اسْتِمَاعِ الْقُرْآنِ وَطَلَبِ الْقِرَاءَةِ مِنْ حَافِظِهِ لِلاِسْتِمَاعِ وَالْبُكَاءِ عِنْدَ الْقِرَاءَةِ وَالتَّدَبُّرِ

chapters.title: The virtue of reciting the Qur’an in prayer and learning it
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ قِرَاءَةِ الْقُرْآنِ فِي الصَّلاَةِ وَتَعَلُّمِهِ ‏

chapters.title: The virtue of Surat al-Kahf and Ayat al-Kursi

chapters.title: The virtue of reciting Qul Huwa Allahu Ahad

chapters.title: The virtue of reciting al-Mu`awwidhatain (the two surahs seeking refuge with Allah)

chapters.title: The times when it is forbidden to offer salat
chapters.title_arabic: باب الأَوْقَاتِ الَّتِي نُهِيَ عَنِ الصَّلاَةِ، فِيهَا ‏.‏

chapters.title: The Supplication When Praying For Rain

chapters.title: Seeking Refuge With Allah When Seeing Wind And Dark Clouds, And Rejoicing At The Rain
chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّعَوُّذِ عِنْدَ رُؤْيَةِ الرِّيحِ وَالْغَيْمِ وَالْفَرَحِ بِالْمَطَرِ ‏‏

chapters.title_arabic: باب فِي رِيحِ الصَّبَا وَالدَّبُورِ ‏‏

chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا عُرِضَ عَلَى النَّبِيِّ صلى الله عليه وسلم فِي صَلاَةِ الْكُسُوفِ مِنْ أَمْرِ الْجَنَّةِ وَالنَّارِ

chapters.title_arabic: باب تَلْقِينِ الْمَوْتَى لاَ إِلَهَ إِلاَّ اللَّهُ ‏

chapters.title_arabic: باب مَا يُقَالُ عِنْدَ الْمُصِيبَةِ ‏

chapters.title: Patience in bearing calamity when it first strikes

chapters.title_arabic: باب التَّشْدِيدِ فِي النِّيَاحَةِ

chapters.title: Shrouding the deceased

chapters.title: Shrouding the deceased well

chapters.title_arabic: باب الإِسْرَاعِ بِالْجَنَازَةِ ‏

chapters.title: The virtue of offering the funeral prayer and following the bier
chapters.title_arabic: باب فَضْلِ الصَّلاَةِ عَلَى الْجَنَازَةِ وَاتِّبَاعِهَا ‏

chapters.title: If one hundred (people) pray for a person, they will intercede for him
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى عَلَيْهِ مِائَةٌ شُفِّعُوا فِيهِ ‏

chapters.title: If forty people pray for a person, they will intercede for him
chapters.title_arabic: باب مَنْ صَلَّى عَلَيْهِ أَرْبَعُونَ شُفِّعُوا فِيهِ ‏‏

chapters.title: The deceased who is spoken well of and the one who is spoken badly of
